Class CookingStartEvent

java.lang.Object
org.bukkit.event.Event
net.sylphian.minecraft.cooking.event.CookingStartEvent
public final class CookingStartEvent extends org.bukkit.event.Event
Fired on the main thread at the start of a new cook cycle. Listeners may reduce getEffectiveCookTime() to apply cook-speed bonuses; the service uses the result for the whole cycle. lastInteractor is the player whose skill levels apply.

  • Constructor Details

    • CookingStartEvent

      public CookingStartEvent(org.bukkit.Location location, CookingRecipe recipe, UUID lastInteractor, int baseCookTime)
      Parameters:
      location - the location of the cooking station block
      recipe - the recipe that just started cooking
      lastInteractor - UUID of the player who last modified the station
      baseCookTime - the recipe's unmodified cook time in ticks

  • Method Details

    • getLocation

      public org.bukkit.Location getLocation()
      Returns:
      the location of the cooking station block

    • getRecipe

      public CookingRecipe getRecipe()
      Returns:
      the recipe that started cooking

    • getLastInteractor

      public UUID getLastInteractor()
      Returns:
      the UUID of the player who last modified the station

    • getEffectiveCookTime

      public int getEffectiveCookTime()
      Returns the effective cook time in ticks for this cycle. Starts at the recipe's base cook time; listeners may reduce it.
      Returns:
      effective cook time in ticks

    • setEffectiveCookTime

      public void setEffectiveCookTime(int effectiveCookTime)
      Sets the effective cook time for this cycle. Must be at least 20 ticks (1 second); the service clamps automatically.
      Parameters:
      effectiveCookTime - cook time in ticks
